data.title: BitMart contracts will be listed on HSBCUSDT and JCIUSDT.
data.description: According to the official announcement, BitMart will launch HSBCUSDT and JCIUSDT perpetual contracts on April 27, 2026 at 17:00 (UTC+8), supporting leverage up to 20x.

data.title: CertiK officially announced its participation in Consensus Miami: Driving Web3 Trust Upgrades with Security.
data.description: CertiK has confirmed its participation in Consensus Miami 2026 as a sponsor. As the world's largest Web3 security company, CertiK plans to deeply engage in industry dialogue and ecosystem building through various activities. During the conference, CertiK will host and co-host two side events, inviting global founders, technology practitioners, and industry representatives to discuss topics such as Web3 security, AI applications, and on-chain infrastructure. Its founder and CEO, Ronghui Gu, will also attend a related roundtable forum to discuss the security and transparency of blockchain and financial infrastructure. In addition, CertiK will have a booth at the venue and conduct several fireside chats with partners, focusing on industry pain points such as institutional adoption, risk visualization, and Web3 compliance implementation. Consensus Miami 2026, hosted by CoinDesk, will be held in Miami, USA, from May 5th to 7th, and is expected to bring together more than 20,000 industry participants from around the world, making it one of the most influential crypto and Web3 industry conferences.

data.title: El-Erian: Middle East Conflict Drives Market Expectations for Prolonged High Interest Rates
data.description: Mohamed A. El-Erian, Chief Economic Advisor at Allianz, has stated that the price shocks resulting from the Middle East conflict have pushed market expectations towards an environment of prolonged high interest rates across almost all systemically important central banks, with the Bank of Japan being the notable exception. According to Jin10, despite recent convergence, the Bank of Japan's policy framework remains unique. El-Erian emphasized that the current situation is not merely a simple price shock but is also accompanied by negative demand shocks from 'second-round effects.' Beyond these direct economic impacts, there is a potential risk of financial instability spreading. He added that all these factors highlight the uncertainty of the outlook: central banks will face a series of difficult trade-offs, and he believes that these decisions will likely—or should—boil down to a stark question: Which mistake can we afford to make that is the least irreversible? For central banks with a single mandate, such as the Bank of England and the European Central Bank, this question is relatively easier to answer. However, for the Federal Reserve, which has a dual mandate, the situation is much more complex.

data.title: Bitcoin Hard Fork Proposal: Paul Sztorc Suggests eCash Launch in August 2026
data.description: Paul Sztorc has proposed a Bitcoin hard fork named eCash, set for August 2026, which aims to replicate BTC balances on a new blockchain. According to NS3.AI, this proposal includes mirroring Satoshi Nakamoto's 1.1 million BTC-equivalent holdings. Sztorc mentioned that less than half of the Satoshi-equivalent eCash coins would be distributed to investors prior to the launch. However, the specific distribution mechanism remains unspecified, and the plan has faced criticism, with some labeling it as theft.

data.title: Solana App Founder Responds to Abuse Allegations
data.description:
Ben Pasternak, founder of the Solana ecosystem application Believe, has responded to allegations made by Evelyn Ha. According to Foresight News, Pasternak stated, "I have never choked anyone in my life. I am not a physically aggressive person, and most people would describe me as very gentle." He further claimed that Evelyn Ha was abusive towards him throughout their relationship and mentioned contacting several of her former partners who reportedly had similar experiences. The couple had sought intervention from a therapist, who was aware of Evelyn's repeated physical abuse towards him. Pasternak emphasized that he possesses substantial documentary evidence, including medical reports, letters from Evelyn admitting to violence, and photos of injuries, all of which will be presented in court.

Previously, Foresight News reported that Ben Pasternak was arrested and charged with second-degree strangulation and third-degree assault, according to the New York Criminal Court.

data.title: Iranian Lawmaker Warns of Regional Security Impact
data.description: An Iranian lawmaker stated on Monday that if Iran's coastal security is threatened, the security of the Gulf of Oman and the Persian Gulf will also be affected. According to Odaily, Fadahossein Maleki, a member of Iran's Parliament National Security and Foreign Policy Committee, accused the United States of hindering negotiation progress. He claimed that U.S. actions, including a maritime blockade, violate the ceasefire agreement. Maleki further asserted that Iran has full control over the Strait of Hormuz and is not seeking negotiations or a ceasefire.

data.title: Moonrock Capital Founder Discusses Role of Insiders in Prediction Markets
data.description: Simon Dedic, founder of Moonrock Capital, shared his views on X regarding the role of insiders in prediction markets. According to Odaily, Dedic noted that while insiders often face criticism, their presence is essential for the functioning of prediction markets. He emphasized that insiders contribute to the accuracy of these markets, which is a fundamental aspect of their operation. Removing insiders, he argued, would not result in a fairer market but rather render it ineffective.

data.title: Japan's Nikkei 225 and South Korea's KOSPI Reach Record Highs
data.description: Japan's Nikkei 225 closed at 60,537.36 on Monday, marking the first time it has surpassed the 60,000 mark. According to NS3.AI, South Korea's KOSPI also set a record, closing at 6,615.03, with a notable increase of 2.15%. Both indexes achieved record closing highs, reflecting strong market performance.

data.title: Both Japanese and South Korean stock markets hit record closing highs, with South Korea's KOSPI index rising 2.15%.
data.description: The Nikkei 225 index closed up 821.18 points, or 1.38%, at 60,537.36 on Monday, April 27; the South Korean KOSPI index closed up 139.4 points, or 2.15%, at 6,615.03 on the same day, both setting new record closing highs. The Nikkei 225 index closed above 60,000 points for the first time. (Jinshi)

data.title: Binance DOGE futures open interest increased to 3.23 billion coins in five days, an increase of nearly $100 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, starting from the afternoon of April 23, Binance futures open interest rose from 2.31 billion DOGE to a peak of 3.23 billion DOGE, an increase of 920 million DOGE, approximately $100 million, representing a growth rate of 39.8%. During the same period, the price of DOGE only rose by a maximum of 5%.

data.title: Moonrock founder: Predicting the market is inseparable from "insiders," and "insider information" makes probabilities more accurate.
data.description: Simon Dedic, founder of Moonrock Capital, wrote on X: "It's interesting that people are always so outraged about 'insiders' on Polymarket, completely ignoring the fact that prediction markets simply cannot function without them. Whether you like it or not, it is these insiders who make prediction markets more accurate; this is their core mechanism. Removing them will not give you a fairer market, but a useless one."

data.title: Iranian lawmaker: If Iran's coastal security is threatened, security along the Gulf of Oman and the Persian Gulf will also be affected.
data.description: According to Iran's International Television, an Iranian lawmaker stated on Monday that if Iran's coastal security is threatened, the security of the Gulf of Oman and the Persian Gulf coast will also be affected. Fadahossein Maleki, a member of the Iranian parliament's National Security and Foreign Policy Committee, accused the United States of obstructing negotiations and stated that US measures, including a naval blockade, violated the ceasefire agreement. He added that Iran has complete control over the Strait of Hormuz and is not seeking negotiations or a ceasefire. (Jinshi)

data.title: GateToken (GT) completed its on-chain burning in Q1 2026, with a total burned value exceeding $1.3 billion.
data.description: According to the official announcement, the on-chain burn of GateToken (GT) in the first quarter of 2026 has been officially completed, with a total of 2,557,729,381,387 GT transferred to the burn address, with a burn value exceeding $20.68 million. Since the launch of the Gate Chain mainnet in 2019, GT has been implementing a continuous burn mechanism, and to date, a total of 187,377,156 GT have been burned, with a total burn value exceeding $1.382 billion (calculated at current prices). The total supply of tokens has been significantly reduced from the initial 300 million to approximately 62.46%. With the launch of Gate Layer and the gradual implementation of native applications such as Gate Perp DEX, Gate Fun, and Meme Go, the usage frequency of GT, as the only gas token of Gate Layer, continues to increase. At the same time, Gate is promoting the coordinated development of infrastructure and applications such as Gate for AI Agent, GateClaw, Gate.AI, and GateRouter, driving a synchronous increase in on-chain transaction and interaction demand. Gate will continue to implement a long-term, stable GT burning mechanism to create a closer positive cycle between the token economic model and real-world usage needs and ecosystem expansion.

data.title: MGBX will launch spot trading of Gnosis (GNO).
data.description: According to official news, MGBX will list Gnosis (GNO) spot trading on April 27, 2026 at 18:00 (SGT). Deposit opening time: April 27, 2026 at 16:00 (SGT). Trading opening time: April 27, 2026 at 18:00 (SGT). Withdrawal opening time: April 29, 2026 at 19:00 (SGT). Gnosis builds decentralized infrastructure for the Ethereum ecosystem.

data.title: ECB to Monitor Oil Prices Amid Stalled US-Iran Talks, Analyst Says
data.description: Germany's Commerzbank interest rate strategist Rainer Guntermann has stated in a report ahead of this week's European Central Bank (ECB) policy meeting that the ECB is expected to continue closely monitoring the high oil prices. According to Jin10, the suspension of U.S. negotiations with Iran and the ongoing stalemate in the Strait situation suggest that oil prices are unlikely to see a significant decline in the short term. Guntermann noted that while this situation will keep the ECB vigilant, it is still too early to consider a rate hike this week.

data.title: White House Correspondents' Dinner Attack Suspect Linked to AI Jesus Image
data.description: According to Odaily, journalist Ken Klippenstein has revealed information about Cole Allen, the suspect in the White House Correspondents' Dinner attack, suggesting a connection to an AI-generated image of Jesus released by U.S. President Donald Trump. Cole Allen, identified as a Protestant, referenced the Book of Revelation's depiction of the 'Antichrist' in relation to the image on social media. Despite Trump's assertion that Allen harbors hatred towards Christians, Allen's manifesto indicates he used biblical teachings to justify his violent actions, arguing that remaining silent in the face of oppression contradicts his faith. Allen, a Caltech graduate, has experience in developing precision hardware related to drones and military targeting systems. The FBI is currently investigating whether Allen acted alone.

data.title: Central Banks to Evaluate Impact of Middle East Conflict Amid Policy Meetings
data.description: Danske Bank analyst Asger Wilhelm Dalsjo stated in a report on April 27 that as the Middle East conflict enters its ninth week, central banks will assess its current and anticipated impacts. According to Jin10, this week, the Bank of Japan, Bank of Canada, U.S. Federal Reserve, Bank of England, and European Central Bank are all scheduled to hold policy meetings. The analyst noted, "Monetary policy decisions will be the main market driver this week." It is expected that these five central banks will maintain their current interest rates, but their evaluations of the economic impact of the Middle East conflict will be closely monitored.

data.title: French Company Capital B Increases Bitcoin Holdings
data.description: French publicly traded company Capital B has increased its Bitcoin holdings by 6 BTC, bringing its total to 2,943 BTC. According to Foresight News, this move reflects the company's ongoing interest in cryptocurrency investments.

data.title: The AI-generated image of Jesus depicting Trump may have been the main motive for the attack at the White House press conference; the suspect had previously cited scripture to denounce Trump as an "antichrist."
data.description: According to information disclosed by journalist Ken Klippenstein, the attack on Cole Allen, the suspect in the White House press conference, may be related to the AI-generated image of Jesus released by Trump. Information shows that Cole Allen, a Protestant, had previously used the image to quote the Book of Revelation about the "Antichrist" on social media. Although Trump claimed that Cole Allen hated Christians, his manifesto shows that Allen was actually using biblical teachings to justify his violence, claiming that remaining silent when others are oppressed violates faith. Cole Allen graduated from Caltech and worked on precision hardware development related to drones and military targeting systems. The FBI is currently investigating whether he acted alone.

data.title: An APE insider suffered a loss of $194,000, closing out $4.58 million in LDO long positions.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, APE insiders have closed out their long positions of 10.26 million LDO tokens (worth approximately $4.58 million), without making a profit and incurring a loss of approximately $194,000.
